stem: Which addresses are valid private addresses? (Choose all that apply.)

explain_lecture.slide: Types of IPv4 Addresses — Public and Private IPv4 Addresses
explain_lecture.quote: As defined in in RFC 1918, public IPv4 addresses are globally routed between internet service provider (ISP) routers. However, private addresses are not globally routable.

stem: What is the purpose of ICMP message?
choices: to inform routers about network topology changes
to ensure the delivery of an IP packet
to provide back response related to issues that are related to the processing of IP packets
to monitor the process of a domain name to IP address resolution
explain_short: ICMP reports back errors and status about IP packet processing (e.g., destination unreachable). It does NOT make IP reliable.

explain_lecture.slide: ICMP Messages — ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 Messages
explain_lecture.quote: Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) provides feedback about issues related to the processing of IP packets under certain conditions.

stem: What is the prefix length notation for the subnet mask 255.255.255.224?

explain_lecture.quote: A prefix length is a less cumbersome method used to identify a subnet mask address. The prefix length is the number of bits set to 1 in the subnet mask.

stem: What type of IPv6 address is FE80::1?
explain_short: FE80::/10 is the link-local prefix. (FF00::/8 = multicast, ::1 = loopback, 2000::/3 = global unicast.)

explain_lecture.quote: IPv6 LLAs are in the fe80::/10 range.

stem: Which transport layer feature is used to guarantee session establishment?
explain_short: SYN → SYN-ACK → ACK establishes a TCP session.

explain_lecture.quote: Step 1: The initiating client requests a client-to-server communication session with the server. Step 2: The server acknowledges the client-to-server communication session and requests a server-to-client communication session. Step 3: The initiating client acknowledges the server-to-client communication session.

stem: A user issues a ping fe80:65ab:dcc1::100 command and receives a response that includes a code of 3. What does this code represent?
choices: communication with the destination administratively prohibited
beyond scope of the source address
explain_short: In ICMPv6 Type 1 Destination Unreachable, code 0 = no route to destination. (The original question wording is ambiguous between ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 codes — Cisco curriculum convention answers 'no route to destination'.)

stem: ______________ are used to identify exactly which protocol and process is requesting or responding to a request.
choices: destination and source logical network addresses
explain_short: Port numbers identify the application/process at each end.

explain_lecture.quote: The combination of the source IP address and source port number, or the destination IP address and destination port number is known as a socket.

stem: What is a characteristic of UDP?
choices: UDP datagrams take the same path and arrive in the correct order at the destination.
Applications that use UDP are always considered unreliable.
UDP reassembles the received datagrams in the order received.
UDP only passes data to the network when the destination is ready to receive the data.
UDP has no way to reorder the datagrams into their transmission order; whatever is received first would be reassembled in that order.
explain_short: UDP doesn't sequence; the application receives data in arrival order.

explain_lecture.quote: UDP does not track sequence numbers the way TCP does. UDP has no way to reorder the datagrams into their transmission order. UDP simply reassembles the data in the order that it was received and forwards it to the application.

stem: Which two types of applications aren't best suited for UDP? (Choose two.)
choices: applications that need data flow control
applications that can tolerate some data loss but require little or no delay
explain_short: UDP doesn't provide flow control or reliability — apps needing those should use TCP.

stem: What is the function of the HTTP POST message?
choices: to request an HTML page from a web server
to send error information from a web server to a web client
to upload content to a web server from a web client
to retrieve client email from an email server using TCP port 110
explain_short: POST uploads data. GET requests pages. POP3 (port 110) retrieves email.

explain_lecture.quote: GET is a client request for data. POST and PUT are used to upload data files and submit form data.

stem: Which three protocols don't operate at the application layer of the TCP/IP model? (Choose three.)
explain_short: ARP works at link/network layer; TCP & UDP are transport layer. FTP and POP3 are application layer.

stem: ______________ is a type of attack where the intruder is looking for wireless network vulnerabilities.
explain_short: Reconnaissance = information gathering / scanning for vulnerabilities.

stem: Which statement is true about Cisco IOS ping indicators?
choices: '!' indicates that the ping was unsuccessful and that the device may have issues finding a DNS server.
'U' may indicate that a router along the path did contain a route to the destination address and that the ping was unsuccessful.
'.' may indicate than a connectivity problem, routing problem, or device security issue exists along the path and that an ICMP destination unreachable message was not provided.
A combination of '.' and '!' indicates that a router along the path did not have a route to the destination address and responded with an ICMP unreachable message.
explain_short: '!' = success, '.' = timeout/no reply, 'U' = destination unreachable received.

stem: How many bits must be borrowed from the host portion of an address to accommodate a router with four connected networks?
explain_short: 2² = 4 subnets. Need 2 borrowed bits.

stem: What block addresses are defined by RFC 1918 for private network use? (Choose three.)
Which two statements correctly describe the concepts of administrative distance and metric? (Choose two.)
choices: Administrative distance refers to the trustworthiness of a particular route
A router first installs routes with higher administrative distances
The value of the administrative distance can not be altered by the network administrator
Routes with the smallest metric to a destination indicate the best path
The metric is always determined based on hop count
The metric varies depending which Layer 3 protocol is being routed
explain_short: Lower AD = more trustworthy. Lower metric = better path. Routers prefer lower AD, then lower metric.

stem:
Refer to the exhibit. Match the description with the routing table entries. The route source protocol for the EIGRP entry, the destination network for the directly connected entry, the metric, the administrative distance, the next hop, and the route timestamp — choose the correct mapping.

R3# show ip route
172.16.0.0/24 is subnetted, 3 subnets
C 172.16.0.0 is directly connected, Serial0/1/0
D 172.16.1.0 [90/21024000] via 172.16.0.1, 00:22:15, Serial0/1/0
C 172.16.2.0 is directly connected, Serial0/1/1
  10.0.0.0/24 is subnetted, 1 subnets
S 10.2.0.0 [1/0] via 172.16.2.2
C 10.3.0.0 is directly connected, FastEthernet0/0
choices: protocol=D, dest=10.3.0.0, metric=21024000, AD=1, next-hop=172.16.2.2, timestamp=00:22:15
protocol=C, dest=172.16.0.0, metric=90, AD=110, next-hop=Serial0/1/0, timestamp=21024000
protocol=S, dest=172.16.1.0, metric=1, AD=90, next-hop=00:22:15, timestamp=172.16.2.2
protocol=O, dest=10.2.0.0, metric=110, AD=21024000, next-hop=Serial0/1/1, timestamp=00:00:00
explain_short: From 'D 172.16.1.0 [90/21024000] via 172.16.0.1, 00:22:15': protocol=D (EIGRP), metric=21024000, timestamp=00:22:15. From 'S 10.2.0.0 [1/0] via 172.16.2.2': AD=1, next-hop=172.16.2.2. The directly-connected destination network is 10.3.0.0.

stem: Which block of addresses would be the minimum to accommodate all of these devices if each type of device was on its own network?
explain_short: /23 = 512 addresses. The minimum block depends on the device counts in the original exhibit; the standard Cisco curriculum answer is /23.

stem: What are two features of the OSPF routing protocol? (Choose two.)
choices: has an administrative distance of 100, used primarily as an EGP
uses Dijkstra's algorithm to build the SPF tree
automatically summarizes networks at the classful boundaries
explain_short: OSPF: Dijkstra SPF, bandwidth-based cost, AD=110 (not 100), classless (no auto-summary).

stem: What is the first step taken by a newly configured OSPF router in the process of reaching a state of convergence?
choices: It floods LSP packets to neighboring routers.
It learns about directly connected links in an active state.
It exchanges hello messages with a neighboring router.
explain_short: OSPF starts by sending Hello packets to discover neighbors and form adjacencies.

stem: What is the metric that is used by OSPF?
explain_short: OSPF metric = cost (= reference bandwidth / interface bandwidth).

stem:
Refer to the exhibit. A network administrator is configuring OSPF for R1 and R2, but the adjacency cannot be established. What is the cause of the issue?

R1: ipv6 address FE80::1 link-local; ipv6 address 2001:DB8:ACAD::1/64; ipv6 ospf 1 area 0
R2: ipv6 address 2001:DB8:ACAD::100/64; ipv6 ospf 10 area 4
choices: The interface s0/0/0 on router R2 is missing a link-local address.
The IP address on router R2 is misconfigured.
explain_short: R1 is in area 0, R2 is in area 4 — for an OSPF adjacency, both routers must be in the same area. (Process IDs can differ between routers; that's locally significant.)

stem: What are two benefits of NAT? (Choose two.)
choices: It makes troubleshooting routing issues easier.
It makes tunneling with IPsec less complicated.
It adds a degree of privacy and security to a network.
explain_short: NAT conserves public IPv4 addresses and hides internal addressing (privacy). It does NOT help IPsec or routing performance — it complicates them.

stem: Which address prefix range is reserved for IPv4 multicast?
explain_short: Class D / multicast range = 224.0.0.0/4.

stem: What is defined by the ip nat pool command when configuring dynamic NAT?
choices: the range of external IP addresses that internal hosts are permitted to access
the pool of available NAT servers
the range of internal IP addresses that are translated
explain_short: ip nat pool defines the inside global address range used for translation. The internal addresses that get translated are defined by an ACL.

stem:
Refer to the exhibit. NAT is configured on RT1 and RT2. The PC is sending a request to the web server. What IPv4 address is the source IP address in the packet between RT2 and the web server?

RT1: Gi0/0=192.168.1.254, S0/0/0=209.165.200.226
RT2: S0/0/0=192.0.2.2, Gi0/0=172.16.1.254/24
PC: Inside local=192.168.1.5, Inside global=209.165.200.245
Web Server: Inside local=172.16.1.10/24, Inside global=203.0.113.10/24
explain_short: Once the packet has crossed RT1's NAT, the source IP is the PC's inside global address (209.165.200.245). It stays that way all the way to the web server — RT2's NAT only translates the destination address, not the source.

stem: Which value in a routing table represents trustworthiness and is used by the router to determine which route to install into the routing table when there are multiple routes toward the same destination?
explain_short: AD ranks the trustworthiness of route sources. Lower AD wins.

stem: What is the purpose of using the 'no shutdown' command after assigning an IP address to a router interface?
explain_short: Router interfaces are administratively down by default; 'no shutdown' brings them up.

stem: Which component is designed to protect against unauthorized communications to and from a computer?
explain_short: Firewalls filter inbound/outbound traffic based on rules.

stem: What is the function of the HTTP GET message?
choices: to request an HTML page from a web server
to send error information from a web server to a web client
to upload content to a web server from a web client
to retrieve client email from an email server using TCP port 110
explain_short: GET retrieves a resource from the server.

explain_lecture.quote: GET is a client request for data. POST and PUT are used to upload data files and submit form data.

stem: What are the ranges of IP addresses that are reserved for private use? (Choose two.)
What are two characteristics shared by TCP and UDP? (Choose two.)
explain_short: Both use port numbers and have a checksum field. Only TCP is connection-oriented and uses 3-way handshake.

stem: What is the TCP mechanism used in connection termination?
explain_short: TCP termination: FIN → ACK → FIN → ACK (4 segments).

stem: Which statements don't characterize UDP? (Choose three.)
choices: UDP provides connection-oriented, fast transport of data at Layer 3.
UDP relies on application layer protocols for error detection.
UDP is a low overhead protocol that does not provide sequencing or flow control mechanisms.
UDP relies on IP for error detection and recovery.
UDP provides sophisticated flow control mechanisms.
explain_short: UDP is connectionless (Layer 4, not 3), has no flow control, doesn't rely on IP for recovery.

stem: A company is expanding its business to other neighbouring buildings. All buildings must remain connected to corporate network at all times. Which network technology is required?
explain_short: Connecting buildings within a city/metropolitan area = MAN.

stem: How many useful IP addresses are available on the 192.168.1.0/24 network?
explain_short: /24 → 256 addresses − 2 (network + broadcast) = 254 usable.

stem: Which command can an administrator issue on a Cisco router to send debug messages to the vty lines?
explain_short: terminal monitor enables console output to the active vty (Telnet/SSH) session.

stem:
Refer to the exhibit. Although the user can access all local LAN resources, the user cannot access any Internet sites by using either FQDN or IP addresses. What could be the cause for this failure?

Windows IPv4 properties: IP address 192.168.10.20, subnet mask 255.255.255.0, default gateway 192.168.10.1, DNS 172.16.20.2 / 172.16.20.3.
Symptom: user reaches LAN OK but cannot reach the Internet by FQDN or by IP.
choices: The DNS server addresses are incorrect.
The default gateway address is incorrect.
The wrong subnet mask was assigned to the workstation.
The workstation is not in the same network as the DNS servers.
explain_short: User can reach LAN (so subnet mask is fine), but cannot reach ANY Internet host even by IP (so it's not DNS — DNS errors only break FQDN). The only thing that breaks all off-LAN traffic is a bad default gateway.

stem: NAT overload is also known as ______. (Do not use abbreviations.)
explain_short: NAT overload maps multiple private IPs to one public IP using different port numbers — that's PAT.

stem: OSPF uses ______ as a metric.
explain_short: OSPF cost = reference bandwidth / interface bandwidth.
